title: SEQUENCE-REVIEW output — client-confirmed slice order after review

sequence_ref.description: Path to 05-sequence.json whose order was presented to the client.

verdict.description: confirmed = client accepts proposed order; reordered = at least one dependency-legal override applied.

review.description: Client review session record — what was presented, how client responded, what overrides applied.

presented_order.description: Proposed order's slice ids in 05-sequence.json position order — what the client saw.

client_response.description: Client response type: confirmed, reordered, deferred, or blocked.

skeleton_pinned.description: Position-1 skeleton slice id; always pinned by RM4.

electable_slices.description: Non-skeleton slice ids offered as build-earlier options (had ordering slack); empty when order is dependency-forced.

overrides.description: Applied legal reorders; each entry records the slice, from_position, to_position, and client rationale; empty on plain confirm.

slice.description: S* identifier of the reordered slice.

client_rationale.description: Client's stated reason for the reorder.

blocked_overrides.description: Requested reorders refused for breaking dependency or moving skeleton; each records the request and reason; empty when none.

reason.description: Why the request was blocked — conflicting depends_on edge or RM4 violation.

needs_followup.description: True iff any blocked override recorded; client asked for something dependencies forbid.

signoff.description: One line capturing client's confirmation or instruction verbatim or faithfully paraphrased.

sequence.description: Confirmed order; same fields as 05-sequence carried verbatim; position renumbered 1..N after any reorder.

id.description: Slice S* identifier carried verbatim from 05-sequence.

skeleton.description: True only on position 1; false elsewhere.

value.description: Value tier carried verbatim from 05-sequence; never re-scored.

retires_risk.description: Risk retired, carried verbatim from 05-sequence, or null.

rationale.description: Carried from 05; rewritten only if reorder changed why this slice sits here.

dependency_check.description: Dependency validation of the confirmed order.

acyclic.description: Always true here — defect order never reaches review.

legal.description: Confirmed order is a valid topological sort of depends_on.

coverage.description: Set coverage: confirmed order permutes but never changes the presented slice set.

confirmed.description: Emitted confirmed order's slice ids; equals presented as a set.

missing.description: Presented ids absent from confirmed; empty on clean run.

added.description: Confirmed ids absent from presented; empty on clean run.
